truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the visible source code.

Items.Notes: The source code under audit contains a hardcoded secret in the configuration namespace (config::security::ADMIN_SECRET) which is used directly in the authenticate function. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.

The safe_string_copy function in the source code under audit does not properly null-terminate the destination buffer when the source string is truncated to fit the destination size. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The validate_config function in the source code under audit uses system() to execute arbitrary commands without input validation or sanitization. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The validate_config function uses system() to execute commands, which is a critical vulnerability as it allows command injection. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.

The source code under audit contains a hardcoded secret in the configuration namespace (config::security::ADMIN_SECRET) which is used directly in the authenticate function. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The source code under audit contains a hardcoded maximum recursion depth (config::limits::MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH) which is used in the factorial computation. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The source code under audit contains a hardcoded maximum recursion depth (config::limits::MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH) which is used in the factorial computation. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The source code under audit contains a hardcoded stack buffer size (config::limits::STACK_BUFFER_SIZE) which is used in the input processing. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.
The source code under audit contains a hardcoded stack buffer size (config::limits::STACK_BUFFER_SIZE) which is used in the input processing. This matches the evidence provided in Run 2.

Items.Key: unsafe use of system() in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|the admin console uses system() to execute commands without proper sanitization or validation, allowing potential command injection attacks.|n/a|replace system() with safer alternatives like execve() for specific commands, or use a whitelist of allowed commands.

unsafe use of system() in threadsafecounter|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|481|482|threading::threadsafecounter::increment|std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;|the threadsafecounter class uses system() in its command execution path, which could allow command injection if the counter is used in a context where user-controlled input is involved.|n/a|remove system() calls. use safer alternatives for command execution.

unsafe use of system() in threadpool|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|514|528|threading::threadpool::~threadpool|template<typename f> void enqueue(f&& task) { { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(queue_mutex_); task_queue_.emplace(std::forward<f>(task)); } condition_.notify_one(); }|the threadpool class uses system() in its command execution path, which could allow command injection if tasks are enqueued with user-controlled input.|n/a|remove system() calls. use safer alternatives for command execution.

unsafe use of strcpy in supercalc|buffer overflow|cwe-676|high|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|692|693|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result); strcpy(result_buffer_, expression.c_str());|the evaluate_expression function uses strcpy() without proper bounds checking, which could lead to buffer overflow vulnerabilities if the input is larger than the destination buffer.|n/a|replace strcpy() with strncpy() or snprintf() to ensure proper bounds checking.

unsafe use of sprintf in supercalc|buffer overflow|cwe-676|high|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|692|693|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|the evaluate_expression function uses sprintf() without proper bounds checking, which could lead to buffer overflow vulnerabilities if the input is larger than the destination buffer.|n/a|replace sprintf() with snprintf() to ensure proper bounds checking.

unsafe use of snprintf in supercalc|buffer overflow|cwe-676|high|0.98|enhanced_calc.cpp|691|692|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|snprintf(formatted_result, sizeof(formatted_result), "%.10g", result); snprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|the evaluate_expression function uses snprintf() without proper bounds checking, which could lead to buffer overflow vulnerabilities if the input is larger than the destination buffer.|n/a|replace snprintf() with safer alternatives like strncpy() or ensure proper bounds checking.
